text.title: use-case-modeling Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for use-case-modeling as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Feature as use case — naming a UC "Display Screen" or "Show Report".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Features describe system behavior, not actor goals. UCs must be named from the actor's perspective: what does the actor achieve?

failure-mode.description: UI nouns in system response — "displays popup", "shows modal", "highlights button".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: UI elements change across design iterations; UCs must use system-behavior verbs (validates, persists, emits, notifies) to remain stable.

failure-mode.description: Main flow with more than 9 steps.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Oversized main flows bundle multiple actor goals. Split into separate UCs; each UC covers one goal.

failure-mode.description: Omitting exception flows — only happy path documented.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Exceptions are where systems fail and users abandon. A UC without exception flows cannot drive test coverage or error-handling design.

failure-mode.description: Missing secondary actors: e.g., no Payment Gateway actor on a payment UC.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: External systems that participate in the interaction belong in the diagram; omitting them produces an incomplete system boundary.

failure-mode.description: Too abstract: "Use System" as a use case name.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: UC names must encode a specific actor goal. Abstract names cannot drive test scaffolding or traceability.
